data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the API information I need. Let me build the implementation:

1. `ChatClientAgent` is created via `chatClient.AsAIAgent(instructions, name, description, tools)` extension method
2. Tools are created using `AIFunctionFactory.Create(delegate)` 
3. The agent runs via `agent.RunAsync(message, session, options)`
4. Iteration limits are controlled via `FunctionInvokingChatClient.MaximumIterationsPerRequest` which is configured when building the chat client pipeline

Let me now write the complete implementation. I'll create:
- `Program.cs` - main entry, DI, agent setup, interactive loop


- `ResearchTools.cs` - both web search and note taking tools

For the web search, I'll use HttpClient to fetch from DuckDuckGo and parse the HTML to extract snippets with simple string processing rather than a full HTML parser to keep dependencies minimal.

Now I'm looking at how to wire function invocation into the agent. The `ChatClientAgent` takes tools in its constructor and uses a `ChatClientBuilder` pipeline internally with `UseFunctionInvocation()`, so I should build the chat client with that middleware first, then wrap it as an agent. I'm checking the `FunctionInvocationDelegatingAgentBuilderExtensions` to see what methods are available, though the output showed it was empty—they might be extension methods on `AIAgentBuilder` instead.

The simplest path forward is to create an `IChatClient` from OpenAI, pass the tools to `chatClient.AsAIAgent()` and let the agent framework handle tool dispatch internally, then run it.

+using System.ComponentModel;
+using Microsoft.Extensions.AI;
+
+namespace ResearchAgent.Tools;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// Provides note-taking capabilities for the research agent.
+/// Stores notes in memory for use during a research session.
+/// </summary>
+public sealed class NoteTakingTool
+{
+    private readonly List<(string Title, string Content, DateTime Timestamp)> _notes = [];
+
+    public IReadOnlyList<AIFunction> AsAIFunctions() =>
+    [
+        AIFunctionFactory.Create(SaveNote, nameof(SaveNote),
+            "Save a research note with a title and content for later reference."),
+        AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetAllNotes, nameof(GetAllNotes),
+            "Retrieve all saved research notes."),
+        AIFunctionFactory.Create(SearchNotes, nameof(SearchNotes),
+            "Search through saved notes by keyword."),
+    ];
+
+    [Description("Save a research note with a title and content for later reference.")]
+    public string SaveNote(
+        [Description("A short title for the note")] string title,
+        [Description("The content/body of the note")] string content)
+    {
+        _notes.Add((title, content, DateTime.UtcNow));
+        return $"Note '{title}' saved successfully (total notes: {_notes.Count}).";
+    }
+
+    [Description("Retrieve all saved research notes.")]
+    public string GetAllNotes()
+    {
+        if (_notes.Count == 0)
+            return "No notes saved yet.";
+
+        return string.Join("\n\n---\n\n",
+            _notes.Select((n, i) =>

+using System.ComponentModel;

+namespace ResearchAgent.Tools;

+/// <summary>
+/// Provides note-taking capabilities for the research agent.
+/// Stores notes in memory for use during a research session.
+/// </summary>
+public sealed class NoteTakingTool
+{
+    private readonly List<(string Title, string Content, DateTime Timestamp)> _notes = [];
+
+    public IReadOnlyList<AIFunction> AsAIFunctions() =>
+    [
+        AIFunctionFactory.Create(SaveNote, nameof(SaveNote),
+            "Save a research note with a title and content for later reference."),
+        AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetAllNotes, nameof(GetAllNotes),
+            "Retrieve all saved research notes."),

+            "Search through saved notes by keyword."),
+    ];
+
+    [Description("Save a research note with a title and content for later reference.")]
+    public string SaveNote(
+        [Description("A short title for the note")] string title,
+        [Description("The content/body of the note")] string content)
+    {
+        _notes.Add((title, content, DateTime.UtcNow));
+        return $"Note '{title}' saved successfully (total notes: {_notes.Count}).";
+    }
+
+    [Description("Retrieve all saved research notes.")]
+    public string GetAllNotes()
+    {
+        if (_notes.Count == 0)
+            return "No notes saved yet.";
+
+        return string.Join("\n\n---\n\n",

+                $"Note {i + 1}: {n.Title}\n{n.Content}"));

+

+            .Where(n => n.Title.Contains(keyword, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
+                     || n.Content.Contains(keyword, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
+            .ToList();
+
+        if (matches.Count == 0)
+            return $"No notes found matching '{keyword}'.";
+
+        return $"Found {matches.Count} note(s) matching '{keyword}':\n\n" +
+               string.Join("\n\n---\n\n",
+                   matches.Select(n => $"**{n.Title}**\n{n.Content}"));

data.content:
Clean build with zero warnings. Here's what was built:

**Architecture** — Uses **Microsoft.Agents.AI** (`ChatClientAgent`) on top of **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient`) with the **OpenAI** provider, following the correct library layering.

**Files created/modified:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `Program.cs` | DI setup, agent construction with tools, interactive REPL loop |
| `Tools/WebSearchTool.cs` | Web search via DuckDuckGo HTML (no API key needed), extracts result snippets |
| `Tools/NoteTakingTool.cs` | In-memory note store with save, get-all, and search capabilities |

**Key guardrails applied:**
- **Iteration limit**: `MaximumIterationsPerRequest = 10` prevents runaway tool loops
- **Temperature 0** for deterministic output
- **MaxOutputTokens = 4096** for cost control
- **API key from environment** (`OPENAI_API_KEY`), never hardcoded
- **Pinned model version** (`gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)

**To run:**
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
dotnet run --project ResearchAgent
```

The agent will prompt for a topic, then autonomously search the web with multiple queries, save findings as notes, and produce a structured summary.
